channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: NASA's stuck astronauts welcome their newly arrived replacements to the space station
item.description: CAPE CANAVERAL, Fla. (AP) — Just over a day after blasting off, a SpaceX crew capsule arrived at the International Space Station on Sunday, delivering the replacements for NASA’s two stuck astronauts. The four newcomers — representing the U.S.
item.dc:creator: MARCIA DUNN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Education Department staff cuts could limit options for families of kids with disabilities
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — For parents of kids with disabilities, advocating for their child can be complicated, time-consuming — and expensive. Changes at the Education Department are likely to make the process even more difficult, advocates for kids with disabilities say.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: What to know about March Madness if you don’t follow college basketball
item.description: No matter how much you know about college basketball — if you’re looking to follow the fun, make your own bracket, or even just understand what all the fuss is about — here are some key things to know.
item.dc:creator: BRIDGET BROWN, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Trump invokes 18th century law to speed deportations, judge stalls it hours later
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal judge barred the Trump administration Saturday from carrying out deportations under a sweeping 18th century law that the president invoked hours earlier to speed removal of Venezuelan gang members from the United States. U.S. District Judge James E.
item.dc:creator: NICHOLAS RICCARDI and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: The Alien Enemies Act: What to know about a 1798 law that Trump has invoked for deportations
item.description: President Donald Trump has invoked the Alien Enemies Act for the first time since World War II, granting himself sweeping powers under a centuries-old law to deport people associated with a Venezuelan gang.
item.dc:creator: Tim Sullivan And Elliot Spagat, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Trump invokes Alien Enemies Act in mass deportation effort
item.description: The president’s move comes hours after a judge temporarily blocked the removal of 5 Venezuelans who the Trump administration says are members of Tren de Aragua.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, KREX 5

item.title: Here's what you need to know about St. Patrick's Day
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — If it's March, and it's green, it must be St. Patrick's Day. The day honoring the patron saint of Ireland is a global celebration of Irish heritage.
item.dc:creator: DEEPTI HAJELA, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Monster storm across the US sparks threat of tornadoes and fire, killing at least 16
item.description: The threat of violent tornadoes in parts of the U.S. proved deadly as well as destructive as whipping winds moved east into the Mississippi Valley and Deep South on Saturday, with at least 16 people killed and scores of homes decimated.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Another state looks to ban this common question drivers are asked every day
item.description: A bill has been introduced into the Colorado legislature that would prohibit officers from asking "Do you know why I pulled you over," and instead tell the driver why they are conducting the traffic stop.
item.dc:creator: Rachel Saurer, KTLA 5

item.title: Bulls title banners removed for season after sustaining damage during Disturbed concert
item.description: All six Bulls championship banners have been removed from the United Center for the rest of the season after sustaining damage during a concert.
item.dc:creator: Andy Koval, KRON4

item.title: Preacher doesn't flinch as activist points apparent gun at his head
item.description: Heart-pounding video shows the moment a pastor had an apparent gun pointed in his face by an activist – but the cool-headed preacher hardly moves a muscle.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Heartwarming photos of Trump with Musk’s son 'Lil X' go viral
item.description: Heartwarming photographs of President Donald Trump walking to Marine Force One with Elon Musk’s son are going viral, with internet users doting over the joyful moment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Worst storms of 2025 will be outside Tornado Alley, severe weather forecast predicts
item.description: Tornadoes can (and have) hit every U.S. state, but the area nicknamed Tornado Alley is probably where your mind goes when you think of dangerous twisters. This year, that's not be the region of highest concern, according to a severe outlook released this month.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Carolina sets date for 5th execution in under 7 months
item.description: A South Carolina inmate is set to become the fifth person to be put to death since the state resumed executions in September after a 13-year pause.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Missing American student: Dominican lawyer puts drowning theory back in play; same beach claimed 4 in January
item.description: A Dominican lawyer believes the possibility that missing Pitt student Sudiksha Konanki drowned is more plausible than some experts have indicated.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Appeals court says Louisiana can carry out the state’s first nitrogen gas execution next week
item.description: BATON ROUGE, La. (AP) — Louisiana's first execution using nitrogen gas is set to move forward as planned next week after a federal appeals court on Friday vacated a preliminary injunction granted by a lower judge. With a March 18 date hastily nearing, attorneys for Jessie Hoffman Jr.
item.dc:creator: SARA CLINE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump demands unprecedented control at Columbia, alarming scholars and speech groups
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Trump administration brushed aside decades of precedent when it ordered Columbia University to oust the leadership of an academic department, a demand seen as a direct attack on academic freedom and a warning of what’s to come for other colleges facing federal scrutiny.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY and JAKE OFFENHARTZ, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Aircraft catches fire after landing in Denver, sending passengers onto wing as smoke engulfs plane
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A fire on an American Airlines plane after it diverted mid-flight and landed at Denver International Airport sent passengers fleeing onto a wing in a fraught evacuation amid billowing clouds of smoke. Airport officials said 12 people were taken to hospitals with minor injuries.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS PEIPERT, SARAH BRUMFIELD, JESSE BEDAYN, COLLEEN SLEVIN and MATTHEW BROWN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Bryan Kohberger case: 911 audio released in Idaho student murders
item.description: Authorities have unveiled the recording of a panicked 911 call placed after the stabbings of four University of Idaho students in November 2022.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: The trend uses her song "Anxiety" and spoofs a scene from "The Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" where Smith's character sneaks up on Ali's, who's dancing with her headphones on, and starts to mimic her.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: FAA 'permanently restricting' Washington helicopter traffic after fatal midair collision near DC airport
item.description: The Federal Aviation Administration announced a "series of steps" it will take to address safety concerns after a fatal midair crash near Ronald Reagan Washington National Airport.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Tampa's Doechii joins Will Smith to recreate viral TikTok trend
item.description: A collaboration decades in the making, Grammy-award winning rapper Doechii joined "Fresh Prince of Bell-Air" alums Will Smith and Tatyana Ali to recreate a viral TikTok trend.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Fanning, WFLA, NBC

item.title: SpaceX mission to replace stuck NASA astronauts launches
item.description: A Falcon 9 rocket carrying the Crew-10 successfully launched from NASA's Kennedy Space Center at 7:03 p.m. ET.
item.dc:creator: Brooke Shafer, KTLA 5

item.title: Trump pledges to 'expose' his enemies in political speech at Justice Department
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump pledged to “expose” his enemies during a norm-breaking political speech Friday at the Justice Department in which he aired a litany of grievances about the criminal cases he faced and vowed retribution for what he described as the “lies and abuses that have o
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, ALANNA DURKIN RICHER and ZEKE MILLER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Senate votes to avoid government shutdown
item.description: The Senate voted mostly along party lines Friday afternoon to pass the House Republican-drafted bill to fund government through September, avoiding a government shutdown only hours before funding was due to lapse. President Trump is expected to sign the bill into law. The final vote was 54-46.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, WFLA, NBC

item.title: More than 50 universities face federal investigations as part of Trump's anti-DEI campaign
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — More than 50 universities are being investigated for alleged racial discrimination as part of President Donald Trump’s campaign to end diversity, equity and inclusion programs that his officials say exclude white and Asian American students.
item.dc:creator: COLLIN BINKLEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Man says social security benefits terminated without warning or explanation
item.description: A local retiree says his Social Security benefits were suddenly suspended without warning — and with no explanation given when he reached out. He worries it may have to do with the place he was born, and ongoing DOGE cutbacks.
item.dc:creator: Spencer Humphrey/KFOR, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Measles confirmed in 3 more states as cases multiply
item.description: More than 300 cases of measles, one of the most contagious viruses in the world, have now been confirmed across more than a dozen U.S. states, the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention announced Friday.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, KRON4

item.title: Judge formally sentences Adnan Syed to time served in 'Serial' podcast case
item.description: ANNAPOLIS, Md. (AP) — A Maryland judge formally sentenced Adnan Syed on Friday to the time he's already served in prison, appearing to finally bring to a close a long-running case with numerous legal twists and turns that received worldwide attention from the true-crime podcast “Serial.
item.dc:creator: BRIAN WITTE, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump administration moves to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma over immigration laws
item.description: DES MOINES, Iowa (AP) — The Trump administration on Friday moved to dismiss lawsuits against Iowa and Oklahoma brought by the Biden administration’s Department of Justice, which challenged the states' immigration laws making it a crime for someone to be in the state if they are in the U.S.
item.dc:creator: HANNAH FINGERHUT, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Key witness in American college student's disappearance tells police how they met, what happened on beach
item.description: Key witness in missing American college student Sudiksha Konanki's disappearance in the Dominican Republic says they were hit by a big wave before she vanished.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump administration resumes detention of immigrant families after Biden-era pause
item.description: McALLEN, Texas (AP) — The Trump administration resumed family detention of immigrants last week in a South Texas facility after a Biden-era pause, a legal nonprofit group providing services to migrant families said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: VALERIE GONZALEZ, Associated Press, ABC 4
